calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To May-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti for (executor) Alan Klein. Patti Breitman for (beneficiary) Peter Greene. Steven Taormina (beneficiary)(via court call) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there are a few issues, that the 13100 affidavit is premature and that the survivorship time has not run yet. Ms. Scagnetti stated that Ms. Breitman is a residuary beneficiary and has also submitted a 13100, that the beneficiaries have been waiting a long time and that they hope to proceed with an order conditioned on the May 10, 2016 date. The Court stated that it does not think the 13100 can be accepted, that it was signed under penalty of perjury, that it is false until May 10, 2016, that it has not seen the 2nd affidavit and asked if it purports to put the assets into the estate. Ms. Scagnetti stated that the 1st affidavit took the assets into the estate and that the examiner requested that the 2nd affidavit by Ms. Wiley be filed. The Court stated that it does not know that it goes to Ms. Wiley yet. Ms. Scagnetti stated that it would go to Ms. Breitman if Ms. Wiley is not available on May 10, 2016. The Court stated that it is not clear how Ms. Breitman would receive. Ms. Scagnetti responded that Ms. Breitman is the remainder beneficiary and beneficiary under Peter Greene' will and provided a copy of the will for the court to review. The Court stated that this presents the problem that the petition before the court was for distribution to Ms. Wiley not Ms. Breitman, that there is a procedural issue that either requires waiting until after May 10, 2016 or amending the petition. Ms. Scagnetti stated that she is concerned as Mr. Taormina and Mr. Pierce have been waiting a long time and need to resolve their living situation. The Court stated that the way this works is that they have to proceed in order, that there are other issues such as payment into the estate of $1400.00 and if it has to happen in advance. Ms. Scagnetti stated that it is for reimbursement of expenses paid by the estate, that it could happen before the order and that Ms. Wiley is able to sign checks for Now Voyager. The Court stated that it needs to happen before the rest of it, that in regards to the request for extraordinary fees there is a lot of money in relationship to Now Voyager and that the question is how much of the work was done for the benefit of the estate. Ms. Scagnetti responded that the initial work was due to the eviction of Now Voyager, then dealing with if the estate were to assume the payment of $20,000.00, that the probate referee took the loan into consideration, that the value of Now Voyager would increase if the loan was paid by the estate, that it was Mr. Greene's desire to not pay the loan, that it was not a personal loan, that there is concern regarding the delay in proceedings, that when Ms. Scagnetti's firm took over it was a year into the case, that the property was put on the market quickly but did not sell until June 8, 2015 and that they were asking for an agreement among the beneficiaries. Mr. Taormina stated that they expressed interest in selling the house in May of 2014, that it did not go on the market until January 2015 and that the delay was due to the previous attorney from Ms. Scagnetti's firm and not caused by the beneficiaries. The accounting petition was continued to May 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to get past the 5/10/16 deadline, for a true affidavit to be filed and that the other matters will be under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Continued To May-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Continued To Apr-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti for and with Alan Klein (via court call). Patti Breitman (beneficiary)in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there are now additional issues with the passing of Mr. Greene. Ms. Scagnetti reported that they have been in discussion regarding an agreement with the beneficiaries, that they have not seen Mr. Greene's will and do not know the size of his estate, that there is a devised interest in the Now Voyager shares, that the beneficiaries are working on an agreement and that Mr. Taormina and Mr. Pierce are willing to accept it if the estate pays the balance on the Citibank loan. Ms. Breitman stated that they cannot find one of the parties, Thomas Orr, that she represents Mr. Greene's estate and that there will be no probate. The Court stated that she will need to file something to obtain authority to act on his will, something that will admit the will or otherwise give her authority and that in regards to the missing beneficiary the court cannot act without an agreement from everyone. Ms. Breitman stated that the missing beneficiary is not part of the agreement and that the will gives her authority. The Court stated that the agreement is not before the court, that if she is not going to be obtaining counsel she should know that estate matters are very formal, that procedures cannot be ignored and that it might make good sense to get counsel. Ms. Scagnetti stated that no objections have been filed, that they would like a comment from the court, that this is the third time they have been here, that they are prepared to amend the petition and confirm the abatement and that they would like a ruling on the extraordinary fee request. The Court stated that it is not inclined to bifurcate, that objections need to be in writing and that it does not know how they plan to proceed. Ms. Scagnetti asked for a time limit on filing objections and stated that they have done a diligent search for Mr. Orr. The accounting petition was continued to March 30, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the court hopes that they are in a position to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Continued To Mar-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti for (petitioner) Alan Klein. William Matheson for and with (beneficiary) Peter Greene. Steven Taormina (beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that petitioner has asked for instructions regarding the allocation of the loan taken out by the business, and asked if the beneficiaries were present to object. Mr. Matheson stated that they object to the extraordinary attorney's fees, but not to the characterization of the loan. The Court asked if anyone was aware of objections of Mr. Pierce or Mr. Orr or if anyone was speaking for them today. Mr. Taormina stated he was here for Mr. Pierce but was told he could not speak for him as he is not an attorney. Mr. Matheson stated that emails had been exchanged. The Court stated that it is looking for a signed statement. Ms. Scagnetti stated that they have requested such many times and that Mr. Matheson has declined. The Court confirmed that Mr. Taormina, Mr. Greene and Mr. Pierce would sign a statement and asked about Mr. Orr. Ms. Scagnetti stated that they have not heard from him and that he only has a small stake in this. The Court confirmed that Mr. Matheson was objecting to the extraordinary fees but nothing else. Mr. Matheson added that he was also objecting to some reimbursements. Mr. Taormina stated that he objects to the extraordinary fees. The Court stated that it is uncomfortable making a determination without something in writing stating an agreement regarding the characterization of the loan, that it will continue this matter to get a statement/agreement on file with the signatures of all of the beneficiaries that they are on board with this and to give time to the objectors to put into writing and file specifically what they object to and why. Objections are due no later than February 17, 2016 and responses to the objections are due no later than February 24, 2016. The court asked who would receive the residue if there is any and was told Alan Klein. The Court asked if Now Voyager is ongoing and was told that it is and that Mr. Greene is in charge. The accounting petition was continued to March 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
